rag_rules: Place component type contracts (assumptions, guarantees) in `annex agree { ... };` blocks within the component type.
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: Use the AADL model as the authoritative source for component structure (names, ports, data types).
When adding range assumptions/guarantees, use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations from the requirement.
Relate visible outputs to visible inputs in guarantees; avoid implementation-only paths in component type contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `eq <variable_name> : bool = <expression>;` to define boolean expressions for range checks.
Ensure AGREE expression types match AADL port types (bool, int, real).
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to directly use AADL properties (e.g., Compute_Execution_Time) as AGREE variables without explicit ports or constants.
Avoid range comparisons or equality checks on Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports unless a numeric scalar representation exists.
